feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Analytics Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP Analytics Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAC Hyperlink doesn't show Account dimension
entry.content:
Hi folks,

When attempting to link to an external URL, Account is not appearing as a selectable dimension despite being present in the model & table we are using to link from.

We would like to avoid needing to solve this through scripting, as this seems like something that should be achievable through the normal functionality, just like all of the other dimensions.
Is it the case that Account is not available in hyperlinks for some reason? If so, why?

entry.title: SAC - Calculated Dimension in Modeler
entry.content:
Greetings,

Does the possibility exist to create a calculated dimension in the modeler itself similar to how we can create calculated dimensions in stories?

The drawback with calculated dimensions in stories is that they are not accessible to other stories or in the excel add-in.

The drawback with calculated columns in import jobs is that if parameters are updated, a reload of historical data is needed, so it's pretty static.

Is there another option to have the following with changing attribute members and not needing to reload historical data?

1. New dimension 3 calculated based on the below logic:

  1. If attribute 1 of dimension 1 is not blank, attribute 1 of dimension 1. Else
  2. If attribute 1 of dimension 2 is not blank, attribute 1 of dimension 2. Else
  3. dimension 

Thank you for your help.

entry.title: I want to know the source of my datasets seen in S4HANA import connection
entry.content: We usually work with S4HANA live connections for all data models built in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). Recently when I was creating datasets, I could see a lot of views listed under the import connection (see attached). I am not sure what is the source of data for these tables or views. Any pointers to the actual source of data. We need to understand the validity of data in these tables (when were they last loaded/refreshed). Without knowing the actual source, I am not able to answer the data validity questions. Thanks,

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Sum
entry.content:
Greetings experts,

I'm working on a project on SAP Analytics Cloud which requires me to put certain values in a table. These values are fed through a live BW connection and that in turn takes the values from a consumption cds view I created.

The issue I am facing lies in the fact that the report i'm trying to create with dynamically change according to a date input filter I have put in place. When filtering for a single PO and for a date range there might be more than one value being fed to the the table and causing SAC to hide the values.

So my question is there any way I can sum all values being returned to the table and then since its already filtering the dates, it would dynamically change said sum?

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Is there any possible way to do a calculation in SAC using two different measures from diff models ?
entry.content: SAC Calculation = measure1 (Model 01) / measure2 (Model 02)

entry.title: How to Create Deeplinks by using Specific Tab URL in ODE Stories?
entry.content:
I am seeking support  with something is so basic but there is no clear guidance.

I would like to use hyperlinks or Deeplink in the e-mails  or MS Office file to link tabs in specific stories.  The issue is in the ODE(Optimized Design Experience) stories,  tabs don't have specific URL every tab  has same URL within the stories. Therefore I can not create deeplinks.

The right question would be how can I get the actual URL of tabs within the a story. In this way I am able to create deeplinks in e-mail or other documents.

It would be great, if you can help.

Best Regards.

#SAC #OptimizedDesignExperience #Hyperlink #Deeplink #Story

Ali

entry.title: SAC Script needed to retrive the selected values of a Hierachie filter parameter/variable
entry.content:
SAC Script needed to retrive the selected values of a Hierachy filter parameter/variable

-got a BW Query,
-the BW Query got a Companycode with a Hierarchy,
- the Companycode Hierachy is for example:
Country Companycode
----------+---------------------
DE        2150
DE        2222
DE       .........
AT         8010
AT        8020
AT        .....
...
REST  1001
REST  1002
REST  ....


The query got a Customer Exit Hierachy Node Variable ZHIERNODEMULTI
for multiple values.

I need  to get the values of selected nodes of the customer exit varable ZHIERNODEMULTI

in a script:
What means in the sample the values of the 0HIERODE :  AT &  DE. 

Anybody got a fitting script ?

Thank You !

Best Martin

entry.title: Performance Issue After Converting SAC Stories to Optimized Design Experience
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I'm sharing an issue we encountered regarding the conversion of SAC stories from the classic design to the optimized design experience.

Tools:

  • BW4HANA  
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (Live Model connection)

 

Issue:

After performing the recommended conversions, we began to notice a decline in performance in the converted stories. Where previously they took only a few seconds to load, the initial load time has now increased significantly.

Has anyone else identified a similar case? Are there known possible causes?

Thank you!

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

entry.title: Remove indentation in Hierarchy SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

 Is it possible in SAC to remove indentation in hierarchies?

Thanks

entry.title: #SAC Set dimension filter value from a dashboard with hierarchy to another table
entry.content:
Hello experts

I have a dashboards where I use an item hierarchy: when I click it I want to pass the selected value to another table (using same model) , to have as result the table filtered with the item selected from first dashboard.

I did many attempts but without result.

I created a similar script over "On select" function of first dashboard:

var sel = Chart_7.getSelections()[0];  // Chart_7 is the first dashboard

Table_10.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("dimItems", "[dimItems].[HryItemcode].[dimItems.ItemCodeMaster]");   // Table_10 is the receiver dashboard

What should I do to achieve the desidered result?

Thank you so much for your help

Adriano

entry.title: #SAC Dynamic percentage calculation in table
entry.content:
Hello experts,

In SAC I have this kind of table where there at max 3 rows, and I want to calculate dinamically the variance % of the last row (in this case 2024 turnover) compared to preview one (in this case 2024 forecast)

I tried to add a row where I calculated in this moment in the formula bar what I need and it works, BUT, this table is dynamic because I can filter with a story filter the year, so in this case I selected 2024, but if select 2023 as example, the table correctly refreshes but the added row is completely wrong.

Does it exist a way to implement this and how? Alternatively how could I approch this matter?

Thank you

Adriano

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud - Get Value by Data Action
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I have a scenario where I have a dummy data row called "Holder," and I want to copy its values to valid data rows. Please see the table below:

Current State     
Dimension 1Dimension 2 Dimension 3 Dimension 4 Dimension 5 Amount (Measure)
      
Holder####150.000
A1B1C1D1E1 
A2B2C2D2E2 
A3B3C3D3E3 
A4B4C4D4E4 
      
      
Desired Outcome    
      
Dimension 1Dimension 2 Dimension 3 Dimension 4 Dimension 5 Amount (Measure)
      
Holder####150.000
A1B1C1D1E1150.000
A2B2C2D2E2150.000
A3B3C3D3E3150.000
A4B4C4D4E4150.000

I've tried various data action coding techniques but haven't achieved the desired outcome. When I use DATA(Dim1=A1)=Resultlookup(Dim1=Holder, Dim2=#), it generates a dataset for every available master data entry. On the other hand, using FOREACH.Booked doesn't work either, as the master data is used in other data rows as well and therefore generates again unvalid data rows

Does anyone have any suggestions?

@N1kh1l

entry.title: Is there a way to apply a selection from a filter in another filter in SAC?
entry.content:
Is there a way to apply a selection (value) made from a page filter applied in another filter in SAC OVM?

If I select ABC in InputControl 1, I want that same value of ABC applied in InputControl 2.

Thank you!

entry.title: Linking Dimensions across different models to pick the current master data status
entry.content:
Hi All,

In SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Planning, we have a requirement to fetch the store status based on the date filtered in the story.

In the below example, Store A is under renovation for Period 1 of 2024 to Period 3 of 2024
Store B is closed in Period 4
Store C was Already Open
Store D is a new store opened in Period 2 of 2024.

User will maintain the data in a custom table

 


Hence, the requirement is that lets assume we are in period 9 of the 2024.

If a person runs the period filter in the dashboard from Period 1 to Period 4, the below store status should be visible in the table, based on the data mentioned in the table

StoreStatus
ARenovation
BClosed
COpen
DNew

In the transaction data being sent to SAC, we have the store code & the posting date being sent.

We do not want to load this store status on the transactional data, since business might change the store status for backdated dates.

E.g., in July 2024, they might change the status of a store of Jan 2024.

Hence, based on the data in the table, we want the system to use a similar formula like VLOOKUP, where system would pick the status mentioned in the above table based on the store code & the period combination.

@N1kh1l if you could please provide any inputs in achieving this.

 

Regards,

Gavin.

entry.title: SAC Script has got no IF (... OR ...) ?
entry.content:
Hi,

PROBLEM:
-A SAC Script with a logic like IF ( condition OR  condition OR ..condition){}  is needed.
-SAC Script or SAP SAC Script Documentation does not provide
info about the possible syntax for  IF (){} at all ?

SOLUTION:
Is there for  SAP Script another logice than if (...=== ...){} at all possible ?
Anybody has got an ideas if at all and how  to run an IF ( ... OR ...) within SAC  Script ? 

 

PROBLEM IN DETAIL:
-have got a BW Query with 10 Variables  Z_QUERY_VAR_01 ... Z_QUERY_VAR_10.
-want  to run operations only on the variables: Z_QUERY_VAR_01 ..._03.
- a SAC Script with an or OR to build the descired logic  ist set up

var filterName = MANAGREP.getDataSource().getVariables();
for ( var j=0; j < filterName.length; j++){
// OR does not seem to work :
if(    ( filterName[j].id === "Z_QUERY_VAR_01")
   or ( filterName[j].id === "Z_QUERY_VAR_02")
   or ( filterName[j].id === "Z_QUERY_VAR_03")
  ){   console.log("++FOUND IT - DO SOMEOPERATIONS ON IT+++");
   }
else { console.log("+++DID NOT FOUND IT - DO NO OPERATIONS ON IT");
}
 
ERROR CREATED
The scriplet raises a browser console error:

Fehler beim Validieren in "MANAGREP.onResultChanged":
Sie haben ein unerwartetes Zeichen eingegeben. Überprüfen Sie Ihr Skript. (8, 46)
Fehlendes Semikolon (8, 87)
Fehlendes Semikolon (8, 131)
Sie haben ein unerwartetes Zeichen eingegeben. Überprüfen Sie Ihr Skript. (8, 133)
Sie haben ein unerwartetes Zeichen eingegeben. Überprüfen Sie Ihr Skript. (12, 4)
Sie haben ein unerwartetes Zeichen eingegeben. Überprüfen Sie Ihr Skript. (16, 0)
"or" ist eine unbekannte Variable. (8, 45)
"or" ist keine Funktion. (8, 45)
"or" ist eine unbekannte Variable. (8, 89)
"or" ist keine Funktion. (8, 89)

LOGICAL BACKGROUND:
For the 3 variables a further processing with 

var filterValuesFISCYEAR02 = MANAGREP.getDataSource().getVariableValues("ZMO_VAR_FISCYEAR02")[0];
switch(filterValuesFISCYEAR02.type){
case VariableValueType.Single:
var singlefilterValue = cast(Type.SingleVariableValue, filterValuesFISCYEAR02 );

...

is required and this does not accept the multiple call of the same code.

Any idea who to work arround of this ?

ThanXs:
Best Martin

entry.title: SAP xP&A HXM Workforce Planning - Tutorial
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am trying my hands on SAP xP&A HXM Workforce Planning - Tutorial with the SAC Trial free version.  I am facing authorization issue in accessing the Application Configuration link of Overview Page. Does this tutorial need a subscription? But I didn't see any such prerequisite.  I have already raised a ticket, but no one replied yet. Could anyone help here?

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Unable to remove version from SAC story table
entry.content:
Hi there I need to remove the version from the table entirely as I'm using a dynamic version filter that users can select to display the version amounts via restricted measures. I'm trying to create a new calculated measure that subtracts the BaseVersion from the ComparativeVersion (across multiple fields, so a simple table 'Subtract' function does not work), however since the version is coming as a required dimension, it displays the calculation in the two separate versions across two columns. I noticed that consultants who previously worked at my company were able to achieve this in a separate story, but when I try to replicate what they have done (in order to see which step is required to remove the Version), I can't seem to even replicate this. Is there something attached to the table that allows Version to be removed completely? I can't seem to see anything different, all selections and filters are the same, only that with the table I'm recreating, the version cannot be removed.

Attached are the two table design panels:

(1) The table where the version has been removed (by consultants who worked at the company in the past)

(2) The table where I'm trying to replicate (1), though I can't remove the version despite the same filtering and selection criteria. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAC write back to SAP datasphere
entry.content:
Hello, 

Can I add to a story based on datasphere a capability of correcting data and sending back to the source via datasphere?
If not, how can I create an exception report to locate my issues?

Thank you
Deb

entry.title: SAC Advance Formula to copy Actuals and Forecast data to another Version
entry.content:
Hello,

I have requirement to copy data of Actuals and Forecast based on selected Source version to Target version in next fiscal year.

For Example

 ActualsForecast
 P1P2P3P4P5P6P7P8P9P10P11P12
J06100100100100100100120120120120120120
             
 ActualsForecast
 P1P2P3P4P5P6P7P8P9P10P11P12
J07100100100100100100120120120120120120
             
             
ResultP1P2P3P4P5P6P7P8P9P10P11P12
J00100100100100100100120120120120120120

For J06, Period 1 to Period 6 are actuals and remaining Periods are Forecast.

For J07 Period 1 to Period 7 are actuals and remaining Periods are Forecast.

Any idea is highly appreciated.

entry.title: SAC Interview Questions (Basic)
entry.content:
Here's a quick questions and answers guide for preparing SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). This guide covers essential aspects of SAC, including its features, capabilities, and best practices. SAP Analytics Cloud is a comprehensive cloud platform designed to combine business intelligence, planning, and predictive analytics in a single solution. It empowers organizations to make data-driven decisions through advanced data visualization, interactive dashboards, and robust analytical tools. Whether you're a seasoned professional or new to SAC, this guide will help you understand the fundamentals and prepare for effective use of the platform.

Questions:

1). What is SAC?

A cloud-based analytics tool by SAP for data analysis, visualization, and decision-making.

SAP analytics cloud is end-to-end cloud solution offered by SAP that integrates Business Intelligence , Planning, Augmented analytics, predictive analytics in a unified platform to make effective decision on organization's data. Basically, a SaaS that combines all analytical capability in a single product for all its users

2). What is the purpose of SAC?

It enables users to access and analyze data from various sources, create interactive dashboards and reports, conduct predictive analytics, and collaborate on insights

 

3). What is Model in SAC?

 "Model" refers to the structured representation of data that is imported or connected to SAC for analysis, visualization, and reporting purposes.

It includes the data schema, dimensions, measures, and relationships necessary for analyzing and visualizing the data effectively within SAC.

 

4). Types of Model?

Live Data Connection :

model establishes a direct connection to an external data source, such as SAP HANA, SAP BW, or other databases. It allows SAC to query and analyze data in real-time without importing it into SAC.

Imported Data Model :

 type of model, data is imported from external sources into SAC and stored within the SAC environment. This allows for offline analysis and provides more flexibility in data manipulation and modeling within SAC.

5). Briefly describe stories in SAP Analytics Cloud

Combine visualizations, charts, and text to communicate insights derived from data analysis.

 

6).In SAP Analytics Cloud, what is the role of predictive analytics?

Predictive analytics allows for predicting future trends by analyzing present and past data. We can uncover valuable insights from the given data by applying robust predictive models. We can also discover relationships between different data with the support of predictive models.

 

7). Name the vital components of an SAP Analytics Cloud.

Digital Boardroom

Analytic applications

Stories

MS Office integration

Enterprise Planning

Business Intelligence

Platform Services

Augmented Analytics

 

8).What do you understand by digital boardroom in SAP Analytics Cloud?

A single presentation in the SAP analytics cloud combines multiple stories. hey give an overview of a business with summaries of different sections in one place.

 

9).List the various data modeling applied in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Relational Data Modeling

E-R Data Modeling

Dimensional Data Modeling

 

10). How is smart prediction performed in SAP Analytics Cloud?

 Smart prediction is made with the help of SAP Machine Learning algorithms. The ML algorithms dig out the relationships between different data and build a formula known as a predictive scenario. We use this formula to predict future trends.'

Three predictive scenarios: regression, classification, and times series

 

11). How is SAP Analytics Cloud advantageous to Tableau?

SAP Analytics Cloud offers better connectivity than Tableau.

Pricing is better for SAP Analytics Cloud than Tableau

Upgrades are challenging in Tableau

SAP Analytics Cloud provides robust BI models to generate effective reports.

12). What is Smart Predict

Smart Predict uses SAP machine learning algorithms to explore relationships in your dataset and build a formula – a Predictive Scenario – to predict future events or trends

13). What is  Smart Insights

With Smart Insights features, business users uncover critical hidden insights from data previously unseen.

 

 14). What is Smart Discovery

Smart Discovery gives an overview of your data by automatically building charts to begin discovering more about your data.

 

15). What is Search to insights

“Search to Insight” is one such feature that brings conversational AI to SAP Analytics Cloud wherein you can create a story by asking questions in natural language.

16). What are dimensions in SAC?

dimensions are attributes that provide context and structure to your data. They typically represent qualitative data, such as product names, customer

17). What are Measures in SAC?

Measures are numerical values representing quantitative data that can be analyzed, aggregated, and compared.

18.) What are the different types of Charts?

 

In SAP Analytics Cloud, there are various types of charts available for visualizing data. Some common types include:

 

**Column Chart**: Represents data using vertical bars, suitable for comparing values across categories.

 

**Bar Chart**: Similar to column charts but with horizontal bars, useful for comparing values within categories.

 

**Line Chart**: Displays data as points connected by lines, suitable for showing trends over time or continuous data.

 

**Area Chart**: Similar to line charts but with the area below the lines filled, emphasizing the magnitude of values over time.

 

**Pie Chart**: Shows data as slices of a circular pie, useful for displaying proportions or percentages of a whole.

 

**Donut Chart**: Similar to pie charts but with a hole in the center, allowing for multiple series comparison.

 

**Combo Chart**: Combines different chart types (e.g., columns and lines) in a single chart, useful for comparing different data types simultaneously.

 

**Scatter Plot**: Represents individual data points on a two-dimensional plane, useful for identifying relationships or correlations between variables.

 

**Bubble Chart**: Similar to scatter plots but with bubbles of varying sizes representing additional data dimensions.

 

**Treemap**: Displays hierarchical data as nested rectangles, with the size of each rectangle representing a measure.

 

**Heat Map**: Represents data using colors in a matrix format, useful for highlighting patterns or trends in large datasets.

19). What are the types of filters in SAC?

In SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), there are several types of filters that users can apply to analyze data:

Story, page, widgets

**Dimension Filters**: Allow users to filter data based on specific values or ranges within dimensions, such as product categories, regions, or time periods.

 

**Measure Filters**: Enable users to filter data based on specific numerical values or ranges within measures, such as sales revenue, quantities sold, or profit margins.

 

**Time Filters**: Specifically designed for filtering data based on time-related dimensions, such as dates, months, quarters, or years. Users can apply time filters to analyze data within specific time periods.

 

**Input Controls**: Provide interactive filtering options, such as dropdown lists, checkboxes, or sliders, allowing users to select specific values or ranges dynamically.

 

**Page Filters**: Allow users to create multiple pages within a story or report, each with its set of filters applied to analyze data from different perspectives.

 

**Rank Filters**: Enable users to filter data based on ranked values, such as top or bottom performers within a dataset.

 

**Advanced Filters**: Provide more complex filtering options, allowing users to create custom filter conditions using expressions or formulas.

These filters help users customize their data analysis and focus on relevant information based on their specific requirements.

20). What are the different types of pages in SAC?

Responsive, Canvas, Grid, and From Smart Discovery pages

 

21). What is Linked Analysis:

Linked Analysis in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) refers to the capability to create relationships between different visualizations or elements within a story or report. It allows users to interactively explore data across multiple charts, tables, or other visualizations by establishing connections between them.

22). What is Threshold?

A threshold is a predefined limit used to highlight certain conditions they provide visual cues to users for better interpretation and analysis of data in SAC visualizations.

23).What is Geo Map and its types?

 "Geo Map" is a visualization tool that displays geographical data on a map.

Bubble

Choropleth

Heatmap

Flow map

24). Difference between Model and Dataset

25).What are public dimensions?

Public dimensions are stand-alone entities that are created independently and can be shared among multiple models.

26). Two types of model:

Analytic and Planning

26).  What is Planning?

SAC Planning is solution offered by SAP to streamline the planning process, budget and forecasting. It integrates both financial and operational planning along with business intelligence to provide a unified platform for effective business decision making.

 

27). How do we start with planning activities in SAC?

Firstly, we create any public dimension if we require in our planning purposes. Define the hierarchy and load the data into them. Then, we create model and add these public dimensions, along with Account dimension and load the data to it. Once this is set we can build a story and use this model to create our planning story.

 

28). What is unbooked data?

Unbooked data is a feature in SAC which shows the cells that contain blank values which can be used for planning. there is no corresponding transactional data for a field.

 

29).What is Data Lock in SAC?

prevents changes to specific data at different stage of the planning process

The option to lock the cell so that others when collaborated do not edit that particular cell. Right-click data lock.

 

30). How to maintain currency table in SAC.

In the modeling section, we have currency conversion tab, where we can import the data.

 

31).What is public dimension?

Stand-alone entities that are created independently and can be shared among multiple models.

Public dimension is entity that can be created by the user which is independent of a model. This can be used in any model

 

 

32). What is Allocation?

Allocation is process of splitting the derived source values into multiple values and storing values in the target data. Allocation is used in planning and data analysis to gain insights.

Spreading : values of source cell are spread across the leaf members . Values taken from higher level of hierarchy to lower level

Distributing : Distribution is another type of allocation that changes values within a hierarchy by distributing amounts from a source member to leaf members of its siblings

Assigning: you can append or overwrite target cells without specifying a source cell

 

 

33). What is variance

Original Budget

Revised Budget

Actual

Planning variance = Original Budget - Revised Budget

Operational Variance = Revised - Actual

 

 

 

34).  What is Data Action

Data actions are a flexible planning tool for making structured changes to

model data, including copying data from one model to another

 

 

35). What is Value driver tree

A value diver tree is a what-if simulation that enables end users to simulate

the outcome of their decisions

SAP Analytics Cloud
